===================
===================
=== BOB DYLAN 1976-04-18 Lakeland (audiowhored).txt
===================
===================
===================

BOB DYLAN and the
Rolling Thunder Revue - part II

Civic Center
Lakeland, Florida
18 April 1976
 

01. Visions Of Johanna
02. If You See Her, Say Hello
03. Vincent van Gogh
04. Weary Blues From Waitin'
05. I'll Be Your Baby Tonight
06. Maggie's Farm
07. One Too Many Mornings
08. Seven Days

09. Railroad Boy
10. Wild Mountain Thyme
11. Blowin' In The Wind
12. I Pity The Poor Immigrant
13. Shelter From The Storm
14. I Threw It All Away
15. Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues
16. Mozambique
17. Going, Going, Gone
18. Lay Lady Lay
19. Silver Mantis 
20. Idiot Wind
21. Knockin' On Heaven's Door
22. Gotta Travel On
 
======

1-2 Bob solo acoustic
3 with Bobby Neuwirth
9-12 with Joan Baez
19 T-bone Burnette vocal
21 with Roger McGuinn
22 ensemble.

======

audiowhore repair project - 1976

source used: LB-200 'off master recording'

Notes about this recording:
- all thanks to the taper for being there and recording this significant concert
- mono recording
- some minor tape damage at the end of Gotta Travel On
- pitch and speed were very close to being true, an indication of the source being very close to the master (as it is decribed)
- i found that the sound altered throughout from being between 0.07 Semitones to 0.16 Semitones off pitch (this is less than 1% off, but when corrected the sound is improved).
- NOTHING from this concert has been officially released.


What i have done:
- adjust pitch and speed for each song individually to correct 
- fix SBEs
- eq mixing to better balance sound
- some hard limit on loud clapping
- some minor digi noises repaired (where possible)
- tape flips, minor dropouts, microphine bumps cleaned/manually removed.

(No noise or hiss reduction used).


Notes about other sources of this show:
LB-2441, LB-4096, LB-5411, LB-6801 and LB-9273 are all sourced from the same recording as LB-200 but are from different generation versions or with different processing.
LB-8652 is a different recording (a second source), has poorer quality but is a recording of the entire 'Revue' performance.
The bootlegs 'UN1976TITLED' (LB-6801) and 'Acoustic Thunder' (LB-10562) have Visions Of Johanna and If You See Her, Say Hello from this show in much poorer quality than LB-200. 
The pitch and speed of these two important songs are significantly too low and slow on those boot sources.
Anyone having only heard those songs from those sources has not properly heard them. 
A perfect example of how pitch and speed changes from tape generations can completely alter/degrade a recording.


========

Rolling Thunder Revue part II ensemble:

Bob Dylan
Scarlet Rivera (violin)
T-bone J. Henry Burnette (guitar & piano)
Steven Soles (guitar)
Mick Ronson (guitar)
Bobby Neuwirth (guitar & vocal)
Roger McGuinn (guitar & vocal)
David Mansfield (steel guitar, mandolin, violin & dobro)
Rob Stoner (bass)
Howie Wyeth (drums)
Gary Burke (congas).

========

If you have enjoyed hearing this repaired version of Lakeland 1976, it is one of many shows that I have repaired and am continuing to work on.
I will keep sharing these on Dime if the following requests are respected:
- Do not post this audio on youtube (this means you Mr Tamborine)
- Do not repost anywhere in mp3 or degraded sound quality (youtube can be worse resolution than mp3!)
- If you feel the need to repost this on other sites, please retain this info file and credit appropriately
- Not for sale or profit, for free sharing only.

========

Thanks to: the taper (please contact me of you're out there), Les Kokay and his 'Songs of the Underground' publication, LosslessBob, Dylanchords.com, and my Cambridge friends.

========

First posted to Dime on the 45th anniversary of this concert.
===================
===================
===================
=== ORIGINAL INFO FILE bd1976-04-18 lakeland (off master).txt
===================
===================
===================
Dylan 
Civic Center
Lakeland, Florida
18 April 1976
 
*source: off master recording
*sound: avg / good
*size: 652 MiB
 
DISC ONE 
1. Visions Of Johanna
2. If You See Her, Say Hello
3. Vincent van Gogh (Robert Friemark)
4. Weary Blues From Waitin' (Hank Williams)
5. I'll Be Your Baby Tonight
6. Maggie's Farm
7. One Too Many Mornings
8. Seven Days
9. Railroad Boy (trad.)
10. Wild Mountain Thyme
11. Blowin' In The Wind
12. I Pity The Poor Immigrant
13. Shelter From The Storm
14. I Threw It All Away
15. Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues
16. Mozambique (Bob DylanJacques Levy/Bob Dylan)
17. Going, Going, Gone

DISC TWO 
1. Lay Lady Lay
2. Silver Mantis (J. Henry Burnett/Fleming)
3. Idiot Wind
4. Knockin' On Heaven's Door
5. Gotta Travel On (Paul Clayton-Larry Ehrlich-David Lazar-Tom Six)
 
************************************************

First Rolling Thunder Revue 2 concert.

Bob Dylan (guitar & vocal),

D1T3-22 Bob Dylan (guitar & vocal), Scarlet Rivera (violin), T-bone J. Henry Burnette (guitar & piano), Steven Soles (guitar), 
	Mick Ronson (guitar), Bobby Neuwirth (guitar & vocal), Roger McGuinn (guitar & vocal), David Mansfield (steel guitar,
	mandolin, violin & dobro), Rob Stoner (bass), Howie Wyeth (drums), Gary Burke (congas).

D1T1 Bob Dylan harmonica.

D1T3 Bob Dylan & Bobby Neuwirth shared vocal.

D1T9-12 Joan Baez (guitar and shared vocal).

D2T2 T-bone J. Henry Burnette vocal, Joan Baez (backup vocal).

D2T4 Bob Dylan & Roger McGuinn shared vocal.

D2T5 shared vocal by Bob Dylan & the group.

BobTalk
This is a song Im gonna try out on you. (before Seven Days)
The great great Joan Baez! (after I Pity The Poor Immigrant)
Well try to do this, weve never done this before (before Going, Going, Gone)
T-bones gonna sing a song called Shasiko. (before Silver Mantis)

Notes

First versions of Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues and Visions Of Johanna during The Rolling Thunder Revue.
Live debuts of If You See Her, Say Hello, Vincent van Gogh, Weary Blues From Waitin', Seven Days, Railroad Boy, 
	Mozambique, Going, Going, Gone, Idiot Wind and Gotta Travel On.
Second live version of I'll Be Your Baby Tonight. the first being played on Isle Of Wight 31 August 1969.
I'll Be Your Baby Tonight, One Too Many Mornings, I Threw It All Away, Going, Going, Gone and Lay Lady Lay all have 
	rewritten lyrics.
===================
===================
===================
